DECLARATION OF STEPHEN M. KAPPOS FILED BY PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. JUDGMENT DEBTOR HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT A STATUTORY EXEMPTION APPLIES. ALTHOUGH THE ADDRESS ON THE NOTICE OF LEVY MAY HAVE BEEN INCORRECT, FAILURE TO SERVE THE WRIT OF EXECUTION, NOTICE OF LEVY, OR LIST OF EXEMPTIONS DOES NOT AFFECT THE VALIDITY OF THE EXECUTION LIEN CREATED BY THE LEVY. THE $3105.26 CURRENTLY BEING HELD BY THE SHERIFF SHALL BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Douglas B. Provencher, Esq., from Provencher & Flatt LLP, 707-284-2380 for creditor and moving party, appearing by telephone

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted.
Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The Judgment debtor's claim of exemption is Denied. Judgment Debtor has failed to show that a statutory exemption applies. Although the address on the notice of levy may have been incorrect, failure to serve the writ of execution, notice of levy, or list of exemptions does not affect the validity of the execution lien created by the levy. The $3105.26 currently being held by the Sheriff shall be released to the Judgment Creditor. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
